Tests JavaScript object behavior with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods in string concatenation.

# Purpose
This code defines an object with custom `valueOf` and `toString` methods, which override the default behavior of these methods in JavaScript. The `valueOf` method returns the string `"1"`, while the `toString` method returns the number `0`. The code then uses an `assert` statement to check if the expression `"1" + object` evaluates to the string `"11"`. This demonstrates how JavaScript uses the `valueOf` method when performing type coercion in a string concatenation operation.
